steps_json: ["Give artemether-lumefantrine per weight band (see dosing card)", "Paracetamol for fever if temp > 38.5°C", "Oral rehydration. Return in 2 days or immediately if convulsions."]
["Give artemether-lumefantrine per weight band", "Paracetamol for fever", "Monitor for danger signs: convulsions, inability to drink, vomiting everything", "Return in 24 hours for follow-up"]

["Refer to health facility — severity signs present", "Give artemether-lumefantrine first dose before referral if child can swallow", "Danger signs to report: repeated vomiting, very high fever, prostration", "Monitor closely during transport"]
["Artemether-lumefantrine 4 tablets at 0, 8, 24, 36, 48, 60 hours", "Paracetamol 1g for fever", "Rest and fluids. Return if no improvement in 48 hours."]

["Artemether-lumefantrine full course", "Paracetamol 1g every 6 hours for fever", "Oral rehydration — at least 2L fluids daily", "Return in 24 hours or immediately if vomiting, confusion, or unable to stand"]

["Refer to health facility — early severe malaria features", "Give rectal artesunate if available before transfer", "Signs: prostration, unable to drink, recent convulsion", "Do not give food — aspiration risk during transport"]

["Emergency referral IMMEDIATELY — IV artesunate required", "Give rectal artesunate suppository before transfer if available", "Place in recovery position if decreased consciousness", "Monitor breathing during transport"]

["Do NOT give oral medication — aspiration risk", "Transfer to health facility IMMEDIATELY", "If convulsing: place in recovery position, protect airway", "Give rectal artesunate suppository if available and transfer will take > 6 hours"]
["Refer to hospital immediately — IV artesunate required", "Monitor consciousness and breathing during transport", "Do not give oral antimalarials"]
["Amoxicillin 40mg/kg/day in 2 doses for 5 days", "Paracetamol for fever", "Encourage fluids and breastfeeding", "Return immediately if breathing worsens, child cannot drink, or lips turn blue"]

["Amoxicillin 40mg/kg/day for 5 days", "Count breaths — refer if > 50/min (infants) or > 40/min (children 1-5)", "Paracetamol for fever and discomfort", "Return in 2 days or immediately if any danger signs"]
["Amoxicillin syrup per weight — see dosing card", "Keep warm, continue breastfeeding", "Return in 2 days or immediately if rapid breathing worsens"]
["Amoxicillin 500mg 3x/day for 5 days", "Paracetamol 1g for fever", "Rest and increased fluids", "Return if shortness of breath at rest, confusion, or no improvement in 3 days"]

["Refer to health facility for assessment", "Give first dose of amoxicillin 500mg before referral", "Monitor breathing rate — severe if > 30/min in adults", "Signs requiring urgent referral: cyanosis, confusion, systolic BP < 90"]

["Refer to health facility — severe pneumonia features present", "Give first dose of amoxicillin before transfer", "Keep child upright or in sitting position", "Count breaths and recount during transport"]

["Refer to hospital IMMEDIATELY", "Give first dose of amoxicillin before transfer if available", "Keep child upright during transport", "Count breaths — severe if > 50/min (infants) or > 40/min (children)"]

["Refer to hospital IMMEDIATELY", "Give first dose of amoxicillin before transfer if available", "Keep child upright during transport", "Count breaths — severe if > 50/min (infants) or > 40/min (children)"]
["Refer to hospital IMMEDIATELY — IV antibiotics required", "Signs of severity: breathing > 30/min, confusion, cyanosis, BP < 90", "Sit patient upright during transport", "Do not delay referral"]

["Oral rehydration salts (ORS) — give 50-100mL after each loose stool", "Continue breastfeeding", "Zinc 20mg daily for 10 days", "Return if child cannot drink, has blood in stool, or condition worsens"]

["ORS 75mL/kg over 4 hours", "Zinc 20mg daily for 10 days", "Reassess after 4 hours — if improved, continue ORS at home", "Return immediately if sunken eyes worsen, skin pinch returns slowly, child cannot drink"]

["Refer to health facility — worsening despite ORS", "Continue ORS sips during transport if child can swallow", "Signs: 6+ stools/day, blood in stool, fever with diarrhea", "Zinc 20mg before referral if not already given"]

["ORS — drink 200-400mL after each loose stool", "Zinc 20mg daily for 10 days if available", "Continue eating — bland foods preferred", "Return if blood in stool, high fever, or unable to keep fluids down"]
["Refer to health facility — signs of dehydration", "Give ORS during transport — 5mL/kg every 30 minutes", "Early severe dehydration: sunken eyes, reduced skin turgor, reduced urine"]

["Refer to health facility IMMEDIATELY — IV fluids urgently needed", "Give ORS sips if child still conscious and able to swallow", "Moderate-severe: lethargic, very sunken eyes, skin pinch goes back slowly"]
["Refer to health facility IMMEDIATELY — IV fluids needed", "Give ORS sips during transport if child can swallow", "Signs of severe dehydration: sunken eyes, skin pinch very slow, lethargic"]
["Refer to hospital immediately — IV fluid replacement needed", "ORS en route if conscious", "Signs: no urine output, unable to stand, extreme weakness, confusion"]

["Refer to OTP (Outpatient Therapeutic Program) for SAM without complications", "MUAC < 11.5cm without medical complications = OTP eligible", "Provide RUTF at OTP enrollment", "Return immediately if child develops fast breathing, fever, or stops eating"]

["Refer to OTP or health facility for SAM assessment", "Check for bilateral pitting edema — if present, refer to ITFC", "MUAC < 11.5cm with some complications: medical clearance required", "Do NOT start RUTF without facility assessment"]

["Do NOT give food or water — aspiration risk", "Transfer to nearest OTP/ITFC immediately", "If unconscious: recovery position, monitor breathing", "MUAC < 11.5cm or visible wasting with edema = SAM"]
["Emergency referral to ITFC", "Continue breastfeeding if conscious", "Do NOT give F-75 or F-100 formula outside facility"]

["Enroll in supplementary feeding program if available", "MUAC 12.5-13.5cm = at risk — monitor monthly", "Dietary counseling: increase frequency of meals, energy-dense foods", "Return if MUAC drops below 12.5cm or child becomes ill"]

["Enroll in OTP (Outpatient Therapeutic Program)", "Provide RUTF (Ready-to-Use Therapeutic Food) per weight", "MUAC 11.5-12.5cm = MAM — monitor weekly", "Return immediately if MUAC drops below 11.5cm"]

["Refer to health facility — MAM with complications", "MUAC 11.5-12.5cm with illness: medical assessment needed", "Check for bilateral edema, infections, or failure to respond to RUTF", "Escalate to ITFC if MUAC < 11.5cm confirmed"]
["Vitamin A 200,000 IU orally today and tomorrow", "Paracetamol for fever if temp > 38.5°C", "Fluids and rest", "Return if rash spreads to eyes, breathing worsens, or child cannot drink"]
["Vitamin A 200,000 IU today and tomorrow", "Paracetamol for fever", "Check eyes daily — treat conjunctivitis with tetracycline eye ointment if present", "Return in 2 days for follow-up"]

["Refer to health facility — worsening measles", "Give Vitamin A 200,000 IU before referral", "Severe features: unable to drink, severe mouth sores, cloudy eyes", "Monitor breathing during transport"]

["Refer to health facility — measles with complications", "Vitamin A 200,000 IU before referral", "Complications: ear infection, mouth sores, conjunctivitis with discharge", "Antibiotic if secondary bacterial infection suspected"]

["Refer to hospital — moderate measles complications", "Give Vitamin A 200,000 IU before transfer", "Complications: pneumonia, severe dehydration, severe malnutrition", "Monitor airway and breathing during transport"]
["Refer to hospital IMMEDIATELY", "Give Vitamin A 200,000 IU before transfer", "Complications: pneumonia, encephalitis, severe eye involvement, severe malnutrition"]

["Refer to health facility for sputum test", "Early TB symptoms: cough > 2 weeks, mild night sweats, slight weight loss", "Do NOT start TB treatment without lab confirmation", "Advise to cover mouth when coughing"]

["Refer to health facility for sputum test", "Symptoms: cough > 2 weeks, night sweats, weight loss, blood in sputum", "Do NOT start TB treatment without lab confirmation", "Isolate patient from others during transport"]

["Refer to hospital — severe TB presentation", "Blood in sputum, respiratory distress, or extreme weight loss = urgent", "Isolate during transport — mask patient if available", "Do NOT start TB drugs without hospital confirmation"]
["Refer to health facility for evaluation", "Child TB signs: failure to thrive, persistent cough, contact with known TB case", "Mantoux test or Xpert MTB/RIF required for diagnosis"]

["Refer for HIV testing and counseling", "Mild symptoms: recurring oral thrush, mild weight loss, skin rashes", "Do NOT disclose HIV status without consent", "Encourage testing — treatment is available and effective"]

["Refer for HIV testing and counseling", "Do NOT disclose HIV status without consent", "If on ART: check adherence and refer to ART clinic", "Opportunistic infections (thrush, PCP, TB) require hospital assessment"]

["Emergency referral — severe HIV complication", "Severe features: unable to walk, severe weight loss, altered consciousness, severe infection", "Do NOT withhold referral due to HIV status", "Treat opportunistic infections en route if possible"]

["Refer to health facility IMMEDIATELY — any neonatal sepsis is urgent", "Early signs: reduced feeding, mild temperature instability, slightly lethargic", "Keep baby warm — skin-to-skin with mother during transport", "All newborn sepsis requires IV antibiotics — treat at facility only"]

["Emergency referral — neonatal sepsis", "Signs: not feeding, abnormal temperature, grunting, bulging fontanelle", "Keep warm during transport — wrap in cloth, skin-to-skin if possible", "Do NOT give any oral medication"]

["Emergency referral IMMEDIATELY — IV antibiotics required", "Signs: not feeding, convulsions, fast/slow breathing, hypothermia, bulging fontanelle", "Keep warm during transport — wrap in cloth, skin-to-skin if possible", "Do NOT give any oral medication"]

["Refer to health facility for bilirubin check", "Physiological jaundice appears day 2-3, resolves by day 14", "Ensure frequent breastfeeding (8-12 times/day)", "Return immediately if baby becomes lethargic or stops feeding"]

["Refer to hospital for phototherapy assessment", "Jaundice visible before 24 hours of age = always emergency", "Jaundice with refusal to feed, lethargy, or high-pitched cry = emergency", "Ensure adequate feeding during transport"]

["Emergency referral IMMEDIATELY — exchange transfusion may be needed", "Severe jaundice: yellow down to palms and soles, extreme lethargy, arching of back", "Kernicterus risk: permanent brain damage if untreated", "Do NOT delay — every hour matters"]

["Paracetamol for fever if temp > 38.5°C", "Test for malaria with RDT if available", "Ensure adequate fluids and rest", "Return in 2 days or immediately if: convulsions, stiff neck, rash, breathing difficulty"]
["Malaria RDT immediately", "Paracetamol for fever", "If RDT positive: treat per malaria protocol", "If RDT negative: seek source — ear, throat, urine", "Return in 24 hours"]

["Refer to health facility — high fever without clear source", "Danger signs: temp > 39.5°C, convulsions, stiff neck, petechial rash, extreme lethargy", "Give paracetamol before transfer", "Malaria RDT during transport if available"]
["Paracetamol 1g for fever", "Malaria RDT if in endemic area", "Adequate fluids and rest", "Return if fever persists > 3 days or new symptoms develop"]
["Not pneumonia — no antibiotics needed", "Paracetamol for fever and discomfort", "Saline nasal drops for congestion", "Encourage fluids", "Return if breathing becomes fast or child cannot drink"]

["Paracetamol for fever", "Count breaths — if > 40/min in child over 1 year, treat as pneumonia", "Saline nasal drops for congestion", "Return in 2 days or immediately if fast breathing or unable to drink"]

["Refer to health facility — severe ARI features", "Count breaths: > 50/min (infants) or > 40/min (children) = refer as pneumonia", "Give amoxicillin first dose before referral", "Signs: stridor, chest indrawing, unable to drink"]

["Clean wound with clean water and soap", "Apply topical antiseptic (gentian violet or chlorhexidine)", "Amoxicillin if spreading or signs of cellulitis", "Return if red streaks appear or fever develops"]
["Clean wound thoroughly with soap and water", "Amoxicillin 40mg/kg/day for 5 days", "Apply antiseptic dressing", "Return in 2 days — refer if spreading redness, fever, or red streaks"]

["Refer to health facility — spreading cellulitis or abscess", "Signs: fever, rapidly spreading redness, red streaks tracking up limb", "Give first antibiotic dose before transfer", "Red streaks indicate lymphangitis — urgent"]
["Clean with soap and water, apply antiseptic", "Amoxicillin 500mg 3x/day for 5 days if infected", "Lance abscess if fluctuant and pointing", "Return if fever, red streaks, or spreading redness"]
["Amoxicillin 500mg 3x/day for 7 days", "Clean and dress wound daily", "Lance abscess if present", "Return in 48 hours — refer if no improvement or spreading"]
["Clean eye with clean cloth and water", "Tetracycline eye ointment 3x/day for 7 days", "Return if vision blurred, eye swollen shut, or no improvement in 3 days"]

["Tetracycline eye ointment 3x/day for 7 days", "Clean discharge with clean cloth 3x/day", "Do not share towels or cloths", "Refer if eye is swollen shut, vision affected, or no improvement in 3 days"]

["Refer to health facility — severe eye infection", "Urgent signs: eye swollen shut, vision loss, pain on eye movement, corneal haziness", "Tetracycline ointment before referral", "Trachoma with corneal involvement = urgent referral"]
["Clean eye with clean cloth and boiled water", "Tetracycline eye ointment 3x/day for 7 days", "Do not rub eye", "Return if vision blurred or no improvement in 3 days"]

["Control bleeding with firm pressure for 10 minutes", "Clean wound thoroughly with soap and water", "Apply antiseptic", "Check tetanus immunization status — give TT if not up to date", "Return if signs of infection appear (heat, pus, red streaks)"]

["Control bleeding with firm direct pressure", "Refer to health facility — wound may need suturing", "Immobilize suspected fractures with improvised splint", "Check tetanus status — administer TT if needed"]

["Apply direct pressure to control bleeding — do not remove", "If possible arterial bleeding: apply tourniquet proximal to wound", "Immobilize fractures with available splint material", "Transfer to hospital IMMEDIATELY"]

["Refer to health facility for assessment", "Warning signs: headache with visual disturbance, swelling of face/hands, reduced fetal movement", "Do NOT give any medication without clinical assessment", "Place in left lateral position if dizzy"]

["Refer to hospital IMMEDIATELY", "Moderate danger signs: heavy bleeding, severe abdominal pain, persistent headache with visual disturbance", "Place in left lateral position during transport", "Do NOT delay for any reason"]

["Refer to hospital IMMEDIATELY", "Danger signs: heavy bleeding, severe headache with visual disturbance, convulsions, no fetal movement", "Place in left lateral position during transport", "Do NOT delay for any reason"]
